dependencies.description: SR-MPLS prefix-SIDs and adjacency-SIDs are advertised via IGP extensions (IS-IS TLV 135/235 sub-TLVs, OSPF Opaque LSA). SR requires an IGP to distribute SID bindings.
dependencies.name: IGP (IS-IS or OSPF) for SID distribution

dependencies.description: SR-TE policies can be distributed via BGP SR Policy SAFI (73) from controllers or between ASBRs. Also used for SRv6 VPN services (VPNv4/v6 with SRv6 SID).
dependencies.name: BGP for SR-TE policy distribution (optional)

dependencies.description: PCE communicates with PCC via PCEP to compute and delegate SR-TE policies. Required for centralized traffic engineering.
dependencies.name: PCEP for centralized path computation (optional)

dependencies.description: All SR-MPLS nodes must have the same SRGB range for index-based prefix-SIDs to resolve to consistent labels.
dependencies.name: Consistent SRGB across SR domain

dependencies.description: IOS-XR requires 'hw-module profile segment-routing srv6 mode' configuration and a reload before SRv6 is operational.
dependencies.name: SRv6 hw-module profile (IOS-XR)

failure_modes.description: The Segment Routing Global Block is full (all label indices allocated) or different nodes have different SRGB ranges, causing prefix-SID label mismatches and forwarding black holes.

resolutions.action: Configure identical SRGB range on ALL nodes in the SR domain
Configure identical SRGB range on ALL nodes in the SR domain
Configure identical SRGB range on ALL nodes in the SR domain
Default SRGB: 16000-23999 (IOS-XR), 16000-23999 (NX-OS) -- verify with 'show segment-routing mpls'
Default SRGB: 16000-23999 (IOS-XR), 16000-23999 (NX-OS) -- verify with 'show segment-routing mpls'
Default SRGB: 16000-23999 (IOS-XR), 16000-23999 (NX-OS) -- verify with 'show segment-routing mpls'
If SRGB needs expansion, plan a maintenance window to change on all nodes simultaneously
If SRGB needs expansion, plan a maintenance window to change on all nodes simultaneously
If SRGB needs expansion, plan a maintenance window to change on all nodes simultaneously
Avoid overlapping SRGB with SRLB (local block) or static label ranges
Avoid overlapping SRGB with SRLB (local block) or static label ranges
Avoid overlapping SRGB with SRLB (local block) or static label ranges
Use 'show segment-routing local-block inconsistencies' (IOS-XR) to find conflicts
Use 'show segment-routing local-block inconsistencies' (IOS-XR) to find conflicts
Use 'show segment-routing local-block inconsistencies' (IOS-XR) to find conflicts

root_causes.cause: SRGB range not configured consistently across all nodes (must be identical for index-based SIDs)
SRGB range too small for the number of prefix-SIDs needed
Static MPLS labels or LDP labels overlapping with SRGB range
Node added to SR domain without matching SRGB configuration

failure_modes.scenario: SRGB exhaustion or range mismatch across domain
symptoms: show segment-routing mpls shows different SRGB range on different nodes
Prefix-SID not appearing in SID database
Traffic black-hole for SR-MPLS forwarded packets
IS-IS/OSPF logs showing SID index out of SRGB range

failure_modes.description: Two or more nodes advertise the same SID index for different loopback prefixes. The conflicting SID is ambiguous and cannot be used for forwarding.

resolutions.action: Identify conflicting nodes: check SID database on any node to see which prefixes claim the same index
Identify conflicting nodes: check SID database on any node to see which prefixes claim the same index
Identify conflicting nodes: check SID database on any node to see which prefixes claim the same index
Reconfigure one of the conflicting nodes with a unique SID index
Reconfigure one of the conflicting nodes with a unique SID index
Reconfigure one of the conflicting nodes with a unique SID index
Maintain a SID allocation spreadsheet or use a controller for SID management
Maintain a SID allocation spreadsheet or use a controller for SID management
Maintain a SID allocation spreadsheet or use a controller for SID management
If using mapping server, ensure no overlap between mapping server entries and locally configured SIDs
If using mapping server, ensure no overlap between mapping server entries and locally configured SIDs
If using mapping server, ensure no overlap between mapping server entries and locally configured SIDs

root_causes.cause: Two routers configured with the same prefix-SID index under different loopbacks
Renumbering or migration leaving stale SID assignments
Lack of centralized SID allocation tracking
Mapping server advertising a SID that conflicts with a locally configured one

failure_modes.scenario: Prefix-SID conflict (duplicate SID index for different prefixes)
symptoms: SID database shows conflict flag for a SID index
IS-IS or OSPF logs warning about duplicate SID
Traffic using the conflicting SID reaches wrong destination
SR-TE policies using the conflicting SID fail validation

failure_modes.description: An SR-TE policy has no valid candidate paths because all segment lists contain at least one SID that cannot be resolved to a forwarding entry. Traffic that should use this policy is either dropped or falls back to IGP.

resolutions.action: Check each SID in the segment list: 'show segment-routing traffic-eng policy detail'
Check each SID in the segment list: 'show segment-routing traffic-eng policy detail'
Check each SID in the segment list: 'show segment-routing traffic-eng policy detail'
Verify MSD capability: platform-specific (typically 6-12 labels for SR-MPLS, varies by ASIC)
Verify MSD capability: platform-specific (typically 6-12 labels for SR-MPLS, varies by ASIC)
Verify MSD capability: platform-specific (typically 6-12 labels for SR-MPLS, varies by ASIC)
Check PCE connectivity: 'show segment-routing traffic-eng pcc'
Check PCE connectivity: 'show segment-routing traffic-eng pcc'
Check PCE connectivity: 'show segment-routing traffic-eng pcc'
For Flex-Algo: verify all transit nodes participate in the required algorithm
For Flex-Algo: verify all transit nodes participate in the required algorithm
For Flex-Algo: verify all transit nodes participate in the required algorithm
Consider using dynamic path computation instead of explicit segment lists for resilience
Consider using dynamic path computation instead of explicit segment lists for resilience
Consider using dynamic path computation instead of explicit segment lists for resilience
Check binding-SID of nested policies if using hierarchical SR-TE
Check binding-SID of nested policies if using hierarchical SR-TE
Check binding-SID of nested policies if using hierarchical SR-TE

root_causes.cause: SID in segment list references a node that is down or unreachable
Maximum SID Depth (MSD) exceeded — label stack too deep for platform hardware
PCE lost connectivity and cannot provide updated paths
Flex-Algo referenced in segment list not enabled on transit nodes
Binding-SID of nested policy not active
Adjacency-SID in explicit path references a link that is down

failure_modes.scenario: SR-TE policy down — segment list unresolvable
symptoms: show segment-routing traffic-eng policy shows policy status DOWN
SR-TE steering not active — traffic taking IGP path instead of engineered path
PCE logs showing path computation failure
BGP routes with color community not resolving via SR policy

failure_modes.description: The SR label stack (MPLS) or SRv6 SID list exceeds the platform's hardware capability to push/process segments. The policy is rejected or packets are dropped.

resolutions.action: Check platform MSD: typically 6 labels for Memory Based, 10-12 for NPU-based (Cisco 8000, NCS-5500)
Check platform MSD: typically 6 labels for Memory Based, 10-12 for NPU-based (Cisco 8000, NCS-5500)
Check platform MSD: typically 6 labels for Memory Based, 10-12 for NPU-based (Cisco 8000, NCS-5500)
Advertise MSD in IGP so PCE can constrain path computation
Advertise MSD in IGP so PCE can constrain path computation
Advertise MSD in IGP so PCE can constrain path computation
Use SRv6 micro-SID (uSID) to reduce header overhead (16-bit per micro-SID vs 128-bit)
Use SRv6 micro-SID (uSID) to reduce header overhead (16-bit per micro-SID vs 128-bit)
Use SRv6 micro-SID (uSID) to reduce header overhead (16-bit per micro-SID vs 128-bit)
Reduce label stack by using binding-SIDs for hierarchical SR-TE
Reduce label stack by using binding-SIDs for hierarchical SR-TE
Reduce label stack by using binding-SIDs for hierarchical SR-TE
Enable 'hw-module profile sr-policy v6-null-label-autopush' (IOS-XR) for SRv6 optimization
Enable 'hw-module profile sr-policy v6-null-label-autopush' (IOS-XR) for SRv6 optimization
Enable 'hw-module profile sr-policy v6-null-label-autopush' (IOS-XR) for SRv6 optimization

root_causes.cause: Explicit segment list has more SIDs than platform supports
TI-LFA backup path requires additional labels beyond the primary path stack
PCE not aware of MSD capability and computing paths that are too deep
SRv6 micro-SID mode not enabled, requiring full 128-bit SIDs (fewer fit per MTU)

symptoms: SR-TE policy rejected with MSD error
Packets with deep label stacks dropped by transit node
PCE unable to find path within MSD constraint
show segment-routing traffic-eng policy shows 'unresolved' for segment lists exceeding MSD

failure_modes.description: SRv6 locator prefix is not being advertised in IGP or not reachable from remote nodes, causing SRv6 SID resolution failures. SRv6 services (VPN, TE) fail.

resolutions.action: Verify SRv6 mode is enabled: 'show segment-routing srv6' (IOS-XR) or 'show segment-routing' (NX-OS)
Verify SRv6 mode is enabled: 'show segment-routing srv6' (IOS-XR) or 'show segment-routing' (NX-OS)
Verify SRv6 mode is enabled: 'show segment-routing srv6' (IOS-XR) or 'show segment-routing' (NX-OS)
On IOS-XR: 'hw-module profile segment-routing srv6 mode base' (or micro-segment) REQUIRES RELOAD
On IOS-XR: 'hw-module profile segment-routing srv6 mode base' (or micro-segment) REQUIRES RELOAD
On IOS-XR: 'hw-module profile segment-routing srv6 mode base' (or micro-segment) REQUIRES RELOAD
Configure locator under 'segment-routing srv6' and reference it in IGP
Configure locator under 'segment-routing srv6' and reference it in IGP
Configure locator under 'segment-routing srv6' and reference it in IGP
Verify MTU: SRv6 adds minimum 40 bytes (IPv6) + 8+16*n bytes (SRH). Ensure path MTU >= 1500 + overhead
Verify MTU: SRv6 adds minimum 40 bytes (IPv6) + 8+16*n bytes (SRH). Ensure path MTU >= 1500 + overhead
Verify MTU: SRv6 adds minimum 40 bytes (IPv6) + 8+16*n bytes (SRH). Ensure path MTU >= 1500 + overhead
Check that all transit nodes have IPv6 forwarding enabled
Check that all transit nodes have IPv6 forwarding enabled
Check that all transit nodes have IPv6 forwarding enabled

root_causes.cause: SRv6 locator not configured or not enabled in IGP
hw-module profile for SRv6 not activated (IOS-XR requires 'hw-module profile segment-routing srv6 mode' and reload)
Locator prefix not redistributed or filtered by IGP policy
MTU too small for SRv6 encapsulated packets (IPv6 + SRH adds 40+ bytes)
Transit nodes not running SRv6 or not forwarding based on SRH

failure_modes.scenario: SRv6 locator not reachable or SID not programmed
symptoms: show segment-routing srv6 sid shows SIDs not operational
SRv6 VPN routes present in BGP but traffic not forwarding
Remote nodes cannot resolve SRv6 SID to a forwarding path
IS-IS not advertising locator prefix in TLV 27 (SRv6 Locator)

failure_modes.description: Topology-Independent Loop-Free Alternate fast-reroute backup paths are not being computed or programmed for protected prefixes, leaving the network without sub-50ms failover for SR paths.

resolutions.action: Enable TI-LFA: 'fast-reroute per-prefix ti-lfa' under IS-IS/OSPF interface (IOS-XR) or globally
Enable TI-LFA: 'fast-reroute per-prefix ti-lfa' under IS-IS/OSPF interface (IOS-XR) or globally
Enable TI-LFA: 'fast-reroute per-prefix ti-lfa' under IS-IS/OSPF interface (IOS-XR) or globally

Verify topology has at least one alternate path to each prefix
Verify topology has at least one alternate path to each prefix
Check SRLB allocation: 'show segment-routing local-block' (IOS-XR)
Check SRLB allocation: 'show segment-routing local-block' (IOS-XR)
Check SRLB allocation: 'show segment-routing local-block' (IOS-XR)
Verify MSD supports the backup label stack depth
Verify MSD supports the backup label stack depth
Verify MSD supports the backup label stack depth
Ensure SR is enabled on all interfaces in the backup path
Ensure SR is enabled on all interfaces in the backup path
Ensure SR is enabled on all interfaces in the backup path

root_causes.cause: TI-LFA not enabled under IGP interface or globally
No alternate path exists in the topology (single-homed node)
SRLB (local block) exhausted — cannot allocate adjacency-SIDs needed for repair path
Platform does not support required MSD for the repair label stack
IGP interface not configured for segment-routing

failure_modes.scenario: TI-LFA backup not computed or not installed
symptoms: show isis fast-reroute shows no backup for protected prefixes
Convergence time exceeds expected sub-50ms for link failures
No repair label stack in CEF/FIB entries for SR prefixes
Link failure causes traffic black-hole until IGP reconverges

resolutions.action: Configure Flex-Algo definition consistently on ALL participating nodes
Configure Flex-Algo definition consistently on ALL participating nodes
Configure Flex-Algo definition consistently on ALL participating nodes

Verify affinity bits on interfaces match the Flex-Algo constraints

Check Flex-Algo topology with IGP show commands to verify full connectivity

root_causes.cause: Flex-Algo definition not configured on all transit nodes
Flex-Algo affinity/metric constraints exclude critical links
IGP not advertising Flex-Algo participation (FAD TLV missing)
Prefix-SID for Flex-Algo not configured on destination node

show isis flex-algo shows algorithm not participating on transit nodes

messages.description: Advertises prefix-SID binding in IS-IS or OSPF
key_fields: Flags (N-flag: node-SID, P-flag: no-PHP, E-flag: explicit-null)
messages.name: IGP Prefix-SID Sub-TLV
messages.when_sent: When segment-routing is enabled on a prefix in the IGP

messages.description: Advertises adjacency-SID for a specific link
key_fields: Flags (B-flag: backup, V-flag: value, L-flag: local)
messages.name: IGP Adjacency-SID Sub-TLV
messages.when_sent: When SR adjacency-SID is assigned to a link (automatic or explicit)

messages.description: Distributes SR-TE policy candidate paths via BGP
key_fields: Segment List (label stack or SRv6 SID list)
messages.name: BGP SR Policy NLRI (SAFI 73)
messages.when_sent: When an SR-TE policy is created/modified and distributed via BGP (e.g., from PCE or controller)

messages.description: PCE provides an explicit SR path to the PCC via PCEP
key_fields: SID type (MPLS label or SRv6 SID)
messages.name: PCEP SR-ERO (Explicit Route Object)
messages.when_sent: In response to PCC path computation request or PCE-initiated policy

messages.description: IPv6 extension header carrying the SRv6 segment list in the data plane
messages.name: SRv6 Segment Routing Header (SRH)
messages.when_sent: Inserted by ingress node when steering traffic into an SRv6 policy

purpose: Source-routed forwarding paradigm where the ingress node steers packets through an ordered list of segments (instructions). SR-MPLS encodes segments as MPLS labels; SRv6 encodes them as IPv6 addresses in a Segment Routing Header (SRH). Eliminates per-flow state in the network core — all forwarding state is in the packet header.

states.description: Segment Routing Global Block (SRGB) has been allocated and is consistent across nodes in the SR domain. Each node maps its prefix-SID index into this label range. This is the operational steady state for SR-MPLS.
states.name: SRGB_ALLOCATED

states.description: SRGB range overlaps with statically configured labels or differs across nodes. Prefix-SID absolute labels will not match across the domain, causing forwarding failures.
states.name: SRGB_CONFLICT

states.description: A prefix-SID or adjacency-SID has been allocated, advertised via IGP, and programmed in the forwarding table. Traffic can be steered using this SID.
states.name: SID_ACTIVE

states.description: Two or more nodes are advertising the same SID index for different prefixes. The conflicting SID is unusable until resolved.
states.name: SID_CONFLICT

states.description: An SR-TE policy is active with at least one valid candidate path. Traffic matching the policy's steering criteria (color + endpoint) is being forwarded along the segment list.
states.name: SR_POLICY_ACTIVE

states.description: All candidate paths for an SR-TE policy are invalid. No segment list can be resolved. Traffic that should use this policy falls back to IGP shortest path or is dropped (depending on steering mode).
states.name: SR_POLICY_DOWN

states.description: SRv6 locator prefix is configured, advertised in IGP, and SIDs under this locator are operational. SRv6 endpoints can be reached.
states.name: SRV6_LOCATOR_ACTIVE

timers.description: Time to compute Topology-Independent Loop-Free Alternate (TI-LFA) backup paths after SPF run
timers.name: TI-LFA Computation
timers.on_expiry: Backup label stacks installed in FIB for fast reroute
timers.tuning_advice: TI-LFA computation time is a function of topology size. Monitor SPF duration if convergence is slow.

timers.description: PCEP session keepalive interval between PCC (router) and PCE controller
timers.name: PCE Keepalive
timers.on_expiry: Send PCEP keepalive to maintain session
timers.tuning_advice: Lower for faster PCE failure detection; use BFD for sub-second detection if supported

timers.description: Maximum time without receiving a PCEP keepalive before declaring PCE dead
timers.name: PCE Dead Timer
timers.on_expiry: PCE session torn down; delegated SR policies revert to local computation or go down
timers.tuning_advice: Set to 4x keepalive timer. If PCE manages critical traffic engineering, consider redundant PCE.

timers.description: Periodic timer for re-evaluating SR-TE policy candidate paths against current topology
timers.name: SR Policy Reoptimization
timers.on_expiry: SR-TE policy segment lists are re-validated; better paths may be selected

os_defaults.notes: Can be triggered manually with 'segment-routing traffic-engineering reoptimize'

timers.tuning_advice: Lower for dynamic environments; event-driven reoptimization is preferred over timer-based

timers.description: Delay before updating RIB after topology change to prevent microloops during convergence. Uses SR to steer traffic along the post-convergence path during the delay window.
timers.name: Microloop Avoidance Delay
timers.on_expiry: RIB updated with new SPF results; SR microloop-avoidance label stack removed
timers.tuning_advice: Should exceed the slowest node's SPF + FIB programming time in the domain

transitions.failure_if_stuck: Check SRGB range consistency across all SR nodes with 'show segment-routing mpls'
transitions.trigger: New node joins domain with overlapping SRGB or static label conflicts
SRGB reconfigured to non-overlapping range and propagated via IGP

transitions.failure_if_stuck: Identify conflicting nodes via SID database and reassign SID indices
transitions.trigger: Another node advertises the same SID index for a different prefix

transitions.failure_if_stuck: Check segment list validity, MSD, and PCE connectivity
transitions.trigger: All segment lists become unresolvable (SID not reachable, MSD exceeded, PCE withdraws path)
At least one candidate path segment list becomes resolvable

transport: SR-MPLS: MPLS label stack; SRv6: IPv6 + SRH (next-header 43)

notes.note: SR-MPLS: IOS-XR is the primary SR platform (Cisco 8000, NCS-5500/5700, NCS-540/560, ASR 9000). SRGB default 16000-23999, configured under 'segment-routing mpls'. All 164+ SR commands in dedicated command family. Supports Flex-Algo (algorithm 128-255), mapping server, TI-LFA, microloop avoidance. Prefix-SID configured under IS-IS/OSPF interface with 'prefix-sid index <N>'. Use absolute labels only when interworking with non-SR nodes.

SRv6 on IOS-XR: REQUIRES 'hw-module profile segment-routing srv6 mode {base|micro-segment}' + RELOAD. micro-segment (uSID) uses compressed 16-bit SIDs for up to 6 uSIDs per 128-bit container — preferred for production. Locator configured under 'segment-routing srv6 locators locator <name>'. SRv6 services: L3VPN (VPNv4/v6), EVPN, TE. Silicon One supports full SRv6 uSID pipeline. Max SID depth varies by platform: NCS-5500 up to 6 SIDs, Cisco 8000 up to 12 SIDs.

SR-TE on IOS-XR: Configured under 'segment-routing traffic-eng'. Supports PCE-delegated and locally-computed policies. Key concepts: policy (color + endpoint), candidate-path (preference), segment-list (ordered SIDs), binding-SID (policy identifier for hierarchical TE). 'show segment-routing traffic-eng policy' is the primary diagnostic. Forwarding validation: 'show segment-routing traffic-eng forwarding policy'.

SR-MPLS: Requires 'feature mpls segment-routing'. Supported on Nexus 9000 with Cisco Cloud Scale (Memory Based) and Cisco Silicon One ASICs. SRGB default 16000-23999. Prefix-SID configured under IS-IS or OSPF interface. TI-LFA supported in 9.3+. NX-OS 10.4 adds SRv6 support. Use 'show segment-routing mpls' for global SR state and 'show segment-routing clients' for protocol registrations.

SR-TE on NX-OS: 'feature mpls segment-routing-traffic-engineering' enables SR-TE policy support. Supports color-based steering with BGP next-hop resolution. PCE integration available via PCEP. Consistency checker: 'show consistency-checker segment-routing mpls' validates label programming in hardware.

SR on SONiC: FRR provides IS-IS SR extension support (prefix-SID, adjacency-SID) in bgp/isisd containers. SRv6 support in FRR 10.x (sonic-202411+) adds uSID. Configuration via CONFIG_DB or vtysh 'router isis' -> 'segment-routing'. Cisco 8000-O with Silicon One natively supports SRv6 uSID in hardware. APPL_DB tables for SR: ROUTE_TABLE entries with MPLS next-hop, SRV6_SID_LIST_TABLE, SRV6_MY_SID_TABLE. Use 'sonic_trace_redis_table' to debug SR programming pipeline. FRR logs SR events to /var/log/frr/isisd.log.
